title: Restore Contacts page create flows (contacts + helpful links)

requirements.summary: Restore the Contacts page “Create contact” flow by wiring the contact create mutation and contacts list query to the backend actor and ensuring the list refreshes immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: From frontend/src/pages/Contacts.tsx, submitting the "New contact" dialog successfully creates a new contact without throwing in the console.
The new contact is persisted in the backend (backend/main.mo) under the caller and is returned by the existing contacts list query after page refresh/login.
After successful creation, the contacts React Query cache is invalidated/refetched (frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts) and the new contact appears in the list without manual reload.
If required fields are missing, the form prevents submission and shows the existing validation feedback (no new UX changes).

file_operations.description: Replace the stubbed Contact hooks with real implementations backed by the actor: enable useGetAllContacts to call the actor contact-list capability when available; implement useCreateContact to call the actor contact-create capability (ensuring required backend parameters like linked task/document arrays are supplied with existing UI defaults, e.g., empty arrays); keep onSuccess invalidation for the ['contacts'] queryKey so the list refetches immediately.

Ensure the submit handler payload matches the updated useCreateContact/useUpdateContact contract (no UI redesign): keep existing validation/toasts; if needed, pass/derive any required fields (e.g., ensure empty strings/arrays are provided via the hook or call-site) so the mutation does not throw and the dialog closes on success.

requirements.summary: Restore the Contacts page “Create link” flow by wiring the helpful-link create mutation and links list query to the backend actor and ensuring the list refreshes immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: From frontend/src/pages/Contacts.tsx, submitting the "New link" dialog successfully creates a new link without throwing in the console.
The new link is persisted in the backend (backend/main.mo) under the caller and is returned by the existing links list query after page refresh/login.
After successful creation, the links React Query cache is invalidated/refetched (frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts) and the new link appears in the list without manual reload.
If required fields are missing, the form prevents submission and shows the existing validation feedback (no new UX changes).

file_operations.description: Replace the stubbed Helpful Link hooks with real implementations backed by the actor: enable useGetAllLinks (or equivalent) to call the actor helpful-links list capability; implement useCreateLink to call the actor helpful-link create capability using the existing form fields; ensure onSuccess invalidation for the ['links'] (or the existing links queryKey used in the file) so the list refetches immediately.

Ensure the “New link” submit handler aligns with the updated useCreateLink/useUpdateLink contract while preserving existing validation/toasts and UI behavior; confirm the mutation success path closes the dialog and clears the form as it currently does.

requirements.summary: Limit changes strictly to restoring create-contact and create-link functionality on the Contacts page without altering unrelated UI or flows.
acceptanceCriteria: No changes are made to unrelated pages/components beyond what is strictly necessary to make contact/link creation work again.
Existing CRUD flows on other pages (e.g., tasks, documents, media, projects, costs) continue to function as before.

file_operations.description: Scope modifications exclusively to the Contacts/Links-related hooks (queries + mutations + cache invalidation) and avoid refactoring or changing behavior of other feature hooks (tasks, projects, media, documents, costs).

Keep Contacts page UI/behavior unchanged except where strictly necessary to make the two create flows succeed (no new features, no layout/styling changes, keep existing validation feedback and dialogs).
